data.title: Tenax Therapeutics names new CFO
data.description:
• Tenax Therapeutics (TENX) appointed Thomas R. Staab II as CFo, effective May 11, 2026, replacing interim CFO Thomas McGauley.
  • Staab brings more than 25 years of financial leadership experience in the healthcare sector. He most recently served as CFO and secretary at LENSAR.
  • Tenax (TENX) also announced inducement equity awards tied to the appointment, including 10,000 restricted stock units and options to purchase 450,000 common shares

data.title: Zscaler's rating cut at Morgan Stanley on growing competition
data.description:
Morgan Stanley downgraded Zscaler's (ZS) rating to Equal-weight from Overweight and reduced the price target to $155 from $200, citing growing competition in Secure Access Service Edge, or SASE.

Shares of the cybersecurity solutions provider dipped about 1% premarket on Tuesday.

Analysts led by Meta Marshall said that when they upgraded Zscaler to Overweight last year, they did so on the view that the company could expand its platform, emerging as a third major platform vendor through the Red Canary acquisition, data security, and digital experience service ZDX.

"While we have seen early traction here, with ZDX bookings up 80% Y/Y to $100mm ARR, we have not yet seen similar traction with Red Canary (as the company looks to incorporate capabilities into Zero Trust Exchange), making it difficult to see the platform story really playing out for now. That puts focus squarely on ZIA/ZPA [Zscaler Internet Access/Zscaler Private Access], which are growing in the mid-teens Y/Y, challenging the ability to sustain 20%+ growth rate once Red Canary becomes inorganic in FY27," said Marshall and her team.

The analysts noted that with the stock down over 35% year-to-date, greater than the security average at about 24%, they acknowledge the market has already priced in this more cautious view. "However, without a material positive catalyst in the near term, we are stepping to the sidelines," said the analysts.

Marshall and her team added that Zscaler remains a clear category leader in Zero Trust and Secure Service Edge, or SSE, with exposure to one of the key secular trends in cybersecurity: SASE. While long-term demand remains robust, the path to growth reacceleration and monetization across the broader platform is proving more complex than initially expected, according to the analysts.

The analysts added that SASE transformations are increasingly large, multi-year infrastructure projects that require coordination across networking, security, and IT teams, resulting in elongated sales cycles.

"At the same time, intensifying competitive dynamics, especially as customers evaluate broader platform vendors with deeper networking capabilities, may be creating incremental friction in large enterprise deals. With more of the market brownfield vs. greenfield at this point, particularly in large enterprise, we see pricing competition stepping up," said the analysts.

data.title: Northrop Grumman is the most oversold industrial stock as Q1 earnings roll on
data.description:
As the market moves into the second week of Q1 earnings, we look at the most oversold stocks in the industrials sector.

Below is a list of industrial stocks ranked by their lowest Relative Strength Index.

The list is topped by Northrop Grumman Corporation (NOC), with an RSI of 23. Republic Services (RSG) and Lockheed Martin (LMT) follow closely behind, with CACI International (CACI) and General Dynamics (GD) rounding out the top five.

The list features several companies from the Aerospace and Defense sub-sector, including RTX Corporation (RTX) and Huntington Ingalls Industries (HII). Environmental and Facilities Services companies are also represented, with Casella Waste Systems (CWST) and Waste Management (WM) among the lowest RSI performers in the group.

The Relative Strength Index is a momentum oscillator that measures the velocity and magnitude of price changes, plotting them on a scale from 0 to 100. RSI compares the magnitude of recent gains to recent losses over a chosen lookback period—typically 14 days.

RSI readings of 70 or above are generally considered a signal that a stock may be overbought and potentially poised for a pullback. An RSI below 30 indicates that a stock may be oversold, signaling that intense selling pressure has potentially driven the price below its fair value.

Here is the list:

  1. Northrop Grumman Corporation (NOC), Market Cap: $86.80B, RSI: 23

  2. Republic Services, Inc. (RSG), Market Cap: $63.43B, RSI: 29

  3. Lockheed Martin Corporation (LMT), Market Cap: $131.81B, RSI: 30

  4. CACI International Inc (CACI), Market Cap: $11.45B, RSI: 31

  5. General Dynamics Corporation (GD), Market Cap: $88.03B, RSI: 32

  6. RTX Corporation (RTX), Market Cap: $251.93B, RSI: 34

  7. Casella Waste Systems, Inc. (CWST), Market Cap: $4.95B, RSI: 35

  8. Waste Management, Inc. (WM), Market Cap: $90.02B, RSI: 37

  9. Huntington Ingalls Industries, Inc. (HII), Market Cap: $14.89B, RSI: 37

  10. Leidos Holdings, Inc. (LDOS), Market Cap: $19.29B, RSI: 37

Industrials ETFs: (XLI), (VIS), (FXR), (IYJ), (FIDU), and (RSPN)

data.title: Healthcare Services GAAP EPS of $0.37 beats by $0.15, revenue of $462.8M beats by $2.86M
data.description:
• Healthcare Services press release (HCSG): Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.37 beats by $0.15.
  • Revenue of $462.8M (+3.4% Y/Y) beats by $2.86M.
  • Reiterates 2026 mid-single-digit revenue growth outlook.
  • Revenue was reported at $462.8 million, a 3.4% increase over the prior year.
    • Segment revenues and margins for Environmental and Dietary Services were reported at $208.3 million and 12.1%, and $254.5 million and 9.0%, respectively.

data.title: Best Buy promotes from within as it sets up a CEO succession plan
data.description:
Best Buy (BBY) announced on Wednesday that Chief Customer, Product and Fulfillment Officer Jason Bonfig will succeed Corie Barry in the CEO position. The appointment will become effective at the end of Q3, when Barry steps down from both the CEO role and the board of directors on October 31. Bonfig will also join the board, and Barry will stay on for six months as a strategic advisor to help ensure a smooth transition.

The board said the appointment followed a broad review of internal and external candidates and reflects confidence that Bonfig can accelerate growth with new ideas and urgency. Chairman David Kenny credited Barry with steady leadership over the past seven years, noting that she guided Best Buy (BBY) through difficult periods while supporting employees, customers, partners, and shareholders. Barry was the second longest serving CEO in the retailer's history.

Bonfig currently oversees the core elements of Best Buy's (BBY) business, including merchandising, e-commerce, marketing, supply chain, Best Buy Canada, and Best Buy Ads, the company’s retail media network. Most recently, he led the creation of the company’s online marketplace in the U.S. and the scaling of its Best Buy Ads business, which are key components of Best Buy's (BBY) growth strategy.

Shares of Best Buy (BBY) edged 0.4% higher in premarket action on Wednesday.

data.title: Stifel Financial Non-GAAP EPS of $1.45 beats by $0.07, revenue of $1.48B beats by $20M
data.description:
• Stifel Financial press release (SF): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.45 beats by $0.07.
  • Revenue of $1.48B (+17.5% Y/Y) beats by $20M.
  • The Company reported net revenues of $1.48 billion, the second best in its history, driven by higher investment banking revenues, asset management revenues, transactional revenues, net interest income, and the recognition of a gain on the sale of Stifel Independent Advisors, LLC, which closed on February 2, 2026.
  • Record asset management revenues, up 12% over the year-ago quarter.
  • Client assets of $538.7 billion, up 11% over the year-ago quarter.
  • Over the last twelve months, recruited trailing twelve-month production totaled approximately $80 million.

data.title: Moody's beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; updates FY26 outlook
data.description:
• Moody's press release (MCO): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $4.33 beats by $0.11.
  • Revenue of $2.1B (+9.4% Y/Y) beats by $40M,and 6% on an organic constant currency2 basis.
  • MCO’s operating margin was 44.3%; MCO’s Adjusted Operating Margin2 expanded by 150 bps to 53.2%.
  • Operating cash flow of $939 million up 24%; Free Cash Flow2 of $844 million up 26%.
  • Accelerated capital returns: $1.7 billion share repurchases and dividends in the first quarter; raised full-year share repurchase guidance to approximately $2.5 billion1.
  • Reaffirmed guidance for full-year 2026 MCO revenue growth1 in the high-single digit percent range and Adjusted Diluted EPS1 within the range of $16.40 to $17.00 vs. $16.68 consensus.
  • Named Christina Kosmowski as CEO of Moody’s Analytics, effective June 2026.

data.title: Boston Scientific cuts full-year outlook despite Q1 beat
data.description:
Shares of Boston Scientific (BSX) traded lower early in the premarket on Wednesday after the MedTech firm lowered its full-year outlook despite reporting better-than-expected financials for Q1 2026.

The company projects its 2026 revenue to grow at roughly 7.0% YoY - 8.5% YoY on a reported basis compared to 10.5% - 11.5% estimated with its Q4 2025 financials in February and ~11% YoY growth implied in the consensus.

Its non-GAAP earnings per share for the year are expected to reach $3.34 - $3.41 compared to $3.43 - $3.49 previously and $3.45 in the consensus.

As for the current quarter, the Marlborough, Massachusetts-based firm expects approximately 5.5% YoY–7.5% YoY growth and $0.82–$0.84 in adjusted EPS compared to ~10% YoY growth and $0.86 in the consensus, respectively.

“We remain focused on executing our long-term strategy and advancing our differentiated pipeline to drive meaningful impact for patients, physicians, and hospital systems,” CEO Mike Mahoney remarked.

However, for Q1, the company did beat Street forecasts, reporting $5.2B in revenue with ~12% YoY growth and $0.80 of adjusted EPS, exceeding the consensus by $30M and $0.01, respectively.

Boston Scientific’s (BSX) Cardiovascular unit led the outperformance, adding $3.5B to the topline with ~14% YoY growth, while Endoscopy net sales reached $736M, implying ~9% YoY growth, exceeding $722.1M in the consensus, according to Bloomberg data.

However, its Urology unit trailed expectations, adding $646M in net sales with a ~2% YoY growth, missing the consensus of $682.9M.

data.title: Sam's Club introduces a one-hour delivery option
data.description:
Sam's Club introduced a one-hour delivery option, according to an update on its website. The membership warehouse chain noted that the delivery offer costs just $10 extra for Plus members or $17 for Club members. Notably, there is no order minimum. The one-hour delivery service is subject to club hours, inventory availability, and geographic coverage.

Sam’s Club had a strong 2025 based on reporting from Walmart (WMT). Sam’s Club U.S. generated $90.2B in net sales in FY25, up from $86.2B in fiscal FY24, and membership income remained a meaningful profit driver for the segment. E-commerce at Sam's Club was up 24% year over year in FY25. 

Looking ahead, at Walmart's (WMT) 2025 Investment Community Meeting, Sam's Club said it aimed to double membership and more than double sales and profit over the next 8 to 10 years, signaling confidence from management and the board in the long-term momentum for the business.

Shares of Walmart (WMT) are up more than 16% on a year-to-date basis as a defensive positioning by investors has provided a boost. Walmart (WMT) is not expected to report first-quarter earnings until the third week of May.

data.title: Avis Budget continues to soar, Michael Burry calls it ‘dumb luck’
data.description:
Avis Budget Group (CAR) shares jumped as much as 9% in premarket trading Wednesday, putting the stock on track for a fifth straight day of gains.

CAR’s stock is up nearly 390% in April, on course to mark its best monthly performance on record. The car rental company has rallied over 565% in the past month alone.

“Avis Budget (CAR) surged 500% in five weeks, driven by an unprecedented short squeeze rooted in extreme float mechanics,” SA columnist Doug Collins said.

Avis Budget Group (CAR) currently has 35.32M shares outstanding and a short interest of 25.55%.

“Two hedge funds, SRS and Pentwater, accumulated over 100% economic interest via shares and swaps, locking up 82% of CAR's float. Short interest exceeded available float, triggering a gamma squeeze as dealers and shorts scrambled to cover, further fueling the rally,” Collins explained.

Responding to the rally, Wall Street investor Michael Burry called the situation “dumb luck” in his Substack.

“That is something we call dumb luck. Through the lens of history from Joe Kennedy’s shoe shiner to this case, speculative markets had never been short of that,” he wrote.
